key_insight: First-principles thermodynamics predicts the Al-V phase diagram more accurately by combining DFT energies with finite-temperature free-energy models, revealing key stable phases and temperature-driven transformations.
engineering_relevance: These results help materials engineers anticipate which Al-V alloy phases will form or persist at service temperatures, supporting phase selection and alloy design.
method: Computed total energies with density functional theory for candidate Al-V phases.
Built composition-continuous free-energy models for the V-rich solid solution and a complex intermetallic phase.
Used statistical mechanics and vibrational free-energy effects to assess finite-temperature stability.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports identification of three V-rich ground states and proposes AlV3 as an intermediate-temperature Al15-structure phase stabilized by anharmonic vibrational free energy.
watch_item: Phase stability depends on the accuracy of the chosen free-energy model and vibrational treatment, especially for partially occupied and complex structures.
paper_title: First-Principles Investigation of the Al-V Phase Diagram
